patternDisplay: bl cl fl gl pl sl
sentenceFrame: The flag is on the sled.
celebration: Leaf #8 — l-blends. Consonant plus /l/ run together (bl · cl · fl · gl · pl · sl). Another fluency leaf on the blend branch.

songs.title: Fl-Fl-Flag!
songs.lyrics:
[Intro]
Fl! Fl! Fl-fl-fl!
(fl… fl… fl…)

[Hook]
Fl! Fl! Two sounds, TOGETHER!
/f/ and /l/ — fl! fl!
Fl-fl-flag! Fl-fl-flag!
FLAG! (wave it!) FLAG!

[Verse 2]
Cl! Cl! /c/ and /l/!
Blend them quick — cl! cl!
Cl-cl-clap! Cl-cl-clap!
CLAP! (clap! clap!) CLAP!

[Verse 3]
Pl! Pl! /p/ and /l/!
Blend them quick — pl! pl!
Pl-pl-plum! Pl-pl-plum!
PLUM! (yum!) PLUM!

[Verse 4]
Sl! Sl! /s/ and /l/!
Blend them quick — sl! sl!
Sl-sl-sled! Sl-sl-sled!
SLED! (zoom!) SLED!

[Bridge]
A potato?! (NO!)
P-p-potato! No blend!
(fl… fl… fl…)

[Outro]
Fl-fl-flag! Cl-cl-clap!
Pl-pl-plum! Sl-sl-sled!
Two sounds together! (blend! blend!)
Fl! Fl! Fl-fl-fl!
songs.sunoStyle: dark trap, 68 bpm, heavy 808 bass, sparse hi-hats, deep whisper-rap verses, kids choir chant on hook, playful spooky, minimal, clean vocals, nursery trap
songs.sunoNotes: l-blends — a consonant plus /l/ said as one quick move, no vowel squeezed between ('fuh-lag' is the error). Each verse takes one blend: fl, cl, pl, sl (bl and gl live in the picture cards). The /l/ is a light tongue-tip tap behind the teeth; keep it crisp. Never sing letter names.

songs.title: The Flag on the Sled
songs.lyrics:
[Intro]
(We can read. We can read!)

[Hook]
The flag is on the sled!
The flag is on the sled!
Where is the flag? (where? where?)
The flag is on the sled!

[Verse 2]
Clap for the flag! (clap! clap!)
The sled can zip and slip!
Slip and slap and clap! (whee!)
The flag is on the sled!

[Verse 3]
Snake can sit on the sled!
Chick can clap and clap!
Slip, sled, slip! (zoom!)
The flag is on the sled!

[Outro]
The flag… the sled… (hush…)
A potato?! (POTATO!)
On the sled?! (zoom, potato, zoom!)
songs.sunoStyle: dark trap, 68 bpm, heavy 808 bass, sparse hi-hats, deep whisper-rap verses, kids choir chant on hook, playful spooky, minimal, clean vocals, nursery trap
songs.sunoNotes: Frame: 'The flag is on the sled.' Every core word (flag/clap/plum shows in the cards; sled) plus slip/slap. Verse 3 puts Snake aboard the sled with the whole cast. Whisper the outro, potato hits loud, then the sled zooms off with the potato riding it.

spreads.artNote: A little red wooden sled alone at the top of a snowy slope in the spotlight, rope curled, ready. Winter hush.
A small triangular flag on a stick planted proudly at the front of the sled, snapping in a breeze. Expedition begins.
Snake — a friendly emerald-green garden snake with large gentle amber eyes — coiled at the helm of the sled gripping the rope, thrilled, the flag over its head.
Chick at the top of the slope clapping her wings together in excitement, cheering the sled off. Snow puffs at her feet.
The sled slides off down the slope, Snake's amber eyes streaming with speed, a spray of snow behind, the flag flattened by the wind.
Close on the little flag whipping and flapping wildly in the rushing air as the sled bombs downhill — three flap-poses in one frame.

spreads.text: Sheep can slip on the sled.
spreads.artNote: Sheep piling on behind Snake mid-run, wool streaming back, delighted and slightly terrified. The sled now overloaded and faster.
The sled skids to a triumphant stop at the bottom in a fan of snow, Chick, Sheep and Snake all clapping and cheering, flag still flying. Victory.

The empty sled sits at the bottom now with a single potato perched where a rider should be, wearing the little flag like a cape, about to slide off again. The cast gapes. No noun printed — the class SHOUTS it.

sentences: The flag is on the sled.
extras: PATTERN TREE: leaf #8 — l-blends. Read flag/clap/plum/sled aloud and hang it on the blend branch.
NO-SCHWA MODELING: fl/cl/pl/sl/bl/gl are single quick moves — 'fl-ag', not 'fuh-lag'. The /l/ is a light tongue-tap behind the teeth.
BLEND LADDER card sort: match each blend to a picture (bl→black, cl→clock, fl→flag, gl→glad, pl→plum, sl→sled). Speed game — recognise, don't sound out letter by letter.
Moveable alphabet: build the blend first as a pair, then add the rest — fl+ag, cl+ap, pl+um, sl+ed.

teacherFocus: Second blend week — l-blends (bl, cl, fl, gl, pl, sl). Same rule as last week: no schwa. 'fl' is one quick move, not 'fuh-luh'. The /l/ is a light tongue-tap behind the teeth; some children over-round it, so keep it crisp and short. Lean on the picture cards and a speed-sort game (match each blend to its picture) — the goal is instant recognition, not laborious letter-by-letter sounding. Snake, Sheep and Chick are all aboard the story sled this week. Hang leaf #8.
